BankingInfo.description:
Banking information — primary operating bank account of the entity.
Treated as sensitive data; exclude from publicly rendered reports unless
the entity has chosen to disclose it (typical for the Imprint / Handels-
register extract). An entity with multiple accounts should list the
authoritative operating account here.

account_number.description:
Domestic account number. Used in jurisdictions where IBAN is not
canonical (e.g. US routing + account, UK sort code + account) or as
a convenience duplicate of the IBAN's trailing digits.

bank_name.description:
Name of the bank or financial institution as it would appear on a
statement (e.g. "Sparkasse Pforzheim Calw", "Mercury", "Qonto").

bic.description: Business Identifier Code (SWIFT/BIC, ISO 9362). 8 or 11 characters.

blz.description:
German Bankleitzahl — 8-digit bank sort code. Legacy identifier,
superseded by BIC for SEPA but still printed on bank statements.

iban.description:
International Bank Account Number (ISO 13616). Stored in its
canonical printable form, with or without spaces; validators should
accept both.

CapitalFlow.description:
Capital flow between entities — narrative description of where equity or
working capital originates and how it is deployed across the group
structure. Intended for human-readable disclosure (investor decks, tax
filings, due diligence), not for machine reconciliation. For
machine-reconcilable money movement, use the relevant finance pillar.

deployment.description:
Deployment of the capital — where it is routed (which subsidiary,
which purpose) and in what form on the receiving side. Free-form
narrative prose. TODO(corpospec): once a controlled vocabulary of
deployment categories stabilises across multiple companies,
introduce a `CapitalFlowDeployment` enum and split the narrative
into (category, note). Until then kept as String to preserve the
expressiveness observed in real-world YAML.

note.description: Additional commentary on timing, conditions, or caveats.

source.description:
Origin of the capital — who contributes it, in what form (equity,
Kapitalrücklage, convertible loan, retained earnings), and for what
amount.

email.description:
Publicly listed contact email for the entity (e.g. imprint / website /
investor relations). Intended for external publication — do not put
internal-only addresses here. RFC 5322 syntax.

phone.description:
Publicly listed contact phone number in E.164 format (e.g.
`+4970839702820`). Intended for external publication.

EntityClass.description:
Classification of an entity's role in the group's brand / product
hierarchy. Distinct from [`EntityType`] (legal vehicle).
Layer 7 insights gate "missing registration number / tax ids / …"
warnings on this discriminator: a `Brand` is not expected to have a
Handelsregister number.

EntityCustom.description:
Typed extension fields for Entity.
Union of all jurisdiction-specific fields observed in .corpospec/ YAML data.

banking.description:
Primary operating bank account. Sensitive — see `BankingInfo`
docs for disclosure guidance.

capital_flow.description:
Current capital-flow disclosure — where the entity's operating
capital originates and how it is deployed across the group.

capital_flow_pre_pivot.description:
Pre-pivot historical capital flow, retained for audit/narrative
continuity after a strategic pivot. Distinct from the current
`capital_flow`.

geschaeftsfuehrer.description:
Display-only list of managing directors (Geschäftsführer), as
PathRefs into `people/**` (e.g. `people/team/maik`). This field is
a convenience surface for the entity record; the authoritative
appointment data (appointment date, representation powers
alleinvertretungsberechtigt / befreit von § 181 BGB, Handels-
register reference) lives in the jurisdiction-de pillar's
`geschaeftsfuehrer` structured records. Keep both in sync.

labor_registration.description:
German labour-registration identifiers (Betriebsnummer et al.)
issued by the Bundesagentur für Arbeit once the entity employs
staff subject to social-insurance contributions.

paid_in_before_filing_eur.description:
Portion of the Stammkapital paid in before the Handelsregister
filing (in euros). For GmbH cash formation this is typically
12 500 (half); for full payment this equals `stammkapital_eur`.

planned_reorganization.description:
Planned corporate reorganisation (e.g. Delaware C-Corp flip,
merger, liquidation) with trigger, action, and commitment level.

planned_subsidiaries.description:
Planned subsidiaries not yet incorporated. Once a subsidiary is
formed, move it into `Entity.subsidiaries` (PathRef list) and
create its own entity record.

primary_role.description:
Free-form label describing the entity's primary operational role
within the group (e.g. "Investment vehicle", "Operating company",
"IP holding"). Display-only — this is NOT a PathRef into the
`roles/` pillar and must not be confused with the formal Role
concept used for governance assignments.

registered_seat_rationale.description:
Narrative rationale for the choice of registered seat (e.g.
investor proximity, talent density, Gewerbesteuer Hebesatz). Used
in diligence and internal governance records.

relationship_to_unstarter.description:
For sibling / parent entities of Unstarter GmbH: description of
the relationship to the main operating entity (sole shareholder,
service provider, holding vehicle, etc.). UNSTARTER-specific
disclosure field; safe to omit for unrelated companies.

sole_shareholder.description:
PathRef of the sole shareholder when the entity has exactly one
(Einpersonen-GmbH / Einpersonen-UG). Either a `people/**` or
`entity/**` PathRef. Omit when there is more than one shareholder —
use the shareholders register (jurisdiction-de pillar) instead.

stammkapital_eur.description:
Registered share capital (Stammkapital) in euros. GmbH minimum is
25 000; UG minimum is 1. Matches the figure recorded in the
Handelsregister.

target_incorporation.description:
Planned milestone dates for an entity that is still in formation
or pre-incorporation. Once the entity is fully registered, these
targets become historical and authoritative dates should live on
the main `Entity` record (`date_of_incorporation`) and in the
jurisdiction-de pillar.

tax_structure.description:
Tax-structure disclosure relevant at the entity level — e.g.
participation exemption, double-taxation-agreement references,
effective tax rates on exit.

vbg_unfallversicherung.description:
Statutory accident-insurance registration with the VBG
(Verwaltungs-Berufsgenossenschaft) — the German employers'
liability insurance for office-based employment.

IncorporationTimeline.description:
Incorporation timeline targets — planned milestones for bringing a
pre-incorporation entity to fully-registered and funding-ready status.
All dates are ISO 8601 (`YYYY-MM-DD`) and represent targets, not
confirmations. Move a target into the main `Entity` record (e.g.
`date_of_incorporation`) once the milestone is achieved.

formation_deed_date.description:
Target date of the notary formation deed (Gründungsurkunde /
Gesellschaftsvertrag signing). For German GmbH/UG this is day zero —
the entity exists "in formation" (`i.G.`) between this date and the
Handelsregister entry.

handelsregister_entry_target.description:
Target date for the entity's entry into the commercial register
(Handelsregister eintragung / HRB number issuance). From this date the
entity has full legal personhood and limited liability.

note.description:
Free-form narrative notes about the incorporation sequencing,
dependencies, or rationale.

steuernummer_target.description:
Target date for the tax identification number (Steuernummer) to be
issued by the responsible Finanzamt. Required before the entity can
legally invoice or reclaim VAT.

wandeldarlehen_ready_target.description:
Target date by which the entity is ready to sign convertible-loan
agreements (Wandeldarlehen). Typically requires: Handelsregister entry
complete, Steuernummer issued, bank account opened, Stammkapital paid
in.

LaborRegistration.description:
Labor registration details (German Betriebsnummer) — identifiers issued
by the Bundesagentur für Arbeit when an entity first employs staff
subject to social-insurance contributions.

betriebsnummer.description:
Eight-digit Betriebsnummer issued by the Bundesagentur für Arbeit.
Uniquely identifies the employer for social-insurance (DEÜV)
reporting.

kundennummer.description:
Customer reference number (Kundennummer) assigned by the Agentur
für Arbeit / Betriebsnummern-Service for correspondence. Distinct
from the Betriebsnummer itself.

wirtschaftsunterklasse.description:
WZ 2008 Wirtschaftsunterklasse code assigned to the employer,
e.g. `62103 (Entwicklung und Programmierung von Anwendungssoftware)`.
Determines the statistical industry classification used for labour-
market reporting.

PathRef.description:
Path-based cross-reference relative to .corpospec/ root.
Pattern: `^[a-z0-9_-]+(/[a-z0-9_.-]+)+$`

PostalAddress.description: Physical address aligned with schema.org PostalAddress.

TaxId.description: Tax identification number with type discriminator.

description: Company profile aligned with schema.org Organization and GLEIF LEI reference data.

address.description:
Primary operating address. May differ from `registered_seat` when
the entity operates from a different location than its legal
seat. Structured per schema.org PostalAddress.

company_purpose.description:
Corporate purpose (Unternehmensgegenstand) — statutory object
clause describing what the entity does. For German GmbH/UG this
is the `Gegenstand des Unternehmens` recorded in the notary deed
and Handelsregister; for US corporations it mirrors the
certificate of incorporation's purpose clause. Free-form prose.

contact.description: Public contact endpoint (email / phone) for external parties.

custom.description:
Jurisdiction-specific and entity-specific extension fields that
do not fit the canonical schema. Kept as a strongly typed union
rather than `serde_json::Value` so report generators can consume
these fields without dynamic parsing.

date_of_incorporation.description:
ISO 8601 date on which the entity was legally formed. For German
GmbH/UG this is the Handelsregister entry date, not the notary
deed date. Optional to accommodate entities in formation; once
incorporation completes, this field should be populated and the
corresponding `target_incorporation` targets become historical.

functional_currency.description:
Functional (reporting) currency of the entity. ISO 4217. Canonical
default for any monetary value elsewhere in the corpus that does not
carry its own currency declaration.

id.description:
PathRef identifier for this entity within the corpus, e.g.
`entity/unstarter-gmbh`. Must be unique across the repository.

jurisdiction.description:
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the jurisdiction under whose
laws the entity is incorporated.

legal_form.description:
Free-form legal-form designation as registered locally (e.g.
"GmbH (Gesellschaft mit beschränkter Haftung)", "UG
(haftungsbeschränkt)", "C-Corp", "LLC"). Complements the
coarse-grained `entity_type` enum with the precise local label
used in official documents.

legal_name.description:
Full legal name as it appears in the commercial register
(Handelsregister / Companies House / State filing), including the
legal-form suffix (e.g. "Unstarter GmbH", "Beevelop UG
(haftungsbeschränkt)", "Acme Corp, Inc.").

lei.description:
Legal Entity Identifier (ISO 17442) — 20-character alphanumeric
code issued by a GLEIF-accredited LOU. Required for entities
subject to financial-market reporting (EMIR, MiFID II, Dodd-
Frank); optional otherwise.

number_of_employees.description: Current employee headcount with the date the snapshot was taken.

parent_organization.description:
PathRef of the parent organisation, when this entity is a wholly
or majority owned subsidiary. For minority investments use the
relevant equity / cap-table pillar instead.

registered_seat.description:
Registered seat (Sitz der Gesellschaft) — the city or municipality
recorded in the commercial register as the entity's legal seat,
e.g. "Berlin", "Dobel, Germany". Free-form by design: the seat is
often just a municipality name, not a full postal address, and
can diverge from the operating address (`address`). For the
structured operating address use `address` instead.

registration_authority.description:
Name of the authority that maintains the registration (e.g.
"Amtsgericht Stuttgart", "Delaware Division of Corporations",
"Companies House"). Paired with `registration_number` to locate
the authoritative public record.

registration_number.description:
Identifier assigned by the registration authority — e.g. German
Handelsregister `HRB 765497`, Delaware file number `7891234`,
UK Companies House number `12345678`. Include the prefix where
it is part of the canonical identifier (e.g. `HRB`).

status.description:
Operational status of the entity (active / pre-incorporation /
dissolved / ...). See `StatusActive` for the full enum.

subsidiaries.description:
PathRefs of subsidiary entities. Mirrors each subsidiary's
`parent_organization` — both sides of the relation should be
kept in sync.

tax_ids.description:
Tax identification numbers issued by various authorities, each
typed (`Steuernummer`, `USt-IdNr`, `EIN`, `DUNS`, etc.) and
optionally scoped to a country.

trade_name.description:
Operating / trade name used for branding and marketing, without
the legal-form suffix (e.g. "Unstarter", "Acme"). Omit when the
trade name equals the legal name.

type.description:
Legal form category — the kind of legal vehicle this entity is
(corporation, LLC, partnership, etc.). Used for schema.org
Organization subtyping and downstream reporting.

url.description: Primary public website of the entity (absolute URL).

title: Entity
